title: notifications-in-app — HTTP + broadcast middleware
description: Two middleware. One HTTP middleware throttles the notifications-in-app REST surface; one broadcast middleware validates channel authorization on WebSocket subscriptions so a user cannot subscribe to another user's private channel.

middleware.description: Per-user rate limit on the mark-read + dismiss + mark-all-read endpoints (60/minute, 500/hour). Prevents fat-fingered clients from flooding writes when the WebSocket connection is flaky and mark-read is retried aggressively.

Broadcast-channel authorization for the private `tenant.{tenant_id}.user.{user_id}` channel. Rejects the subscription if the authenticated Sanctum token does not match the {tenant_id, user_id} in the channel name. Runs before Laravel's default Broadcast::channel() callback so unauthorized subscriptions never enter the auth callback path.
